channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Johnson slams Biden administration’s ‘abject failure’ assisting Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Speaker of the House Mike Johnson slammed the federal government's response to Hurricane Helene, which has left hundreds dead, as an "abject failure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Councilman Joe Borelli on whether Mayor Eric Adams should resign
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams is facing mounting pressure following his indictment, with more federal corruption charges possible. Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Adams should resign, according to a Marist College poll released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Civil rights groups ask to extend voter registration deadlines in hurricane-ravaged states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With registration deadlines looming, Democrats and civil rights groups are asking election officials in the states ravaged by Hurricane Helene to give voters more time. A judge in South Carolina on Friday extended that state's deadline to Oct.
item.dc:creator: AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: County official accuses two supervisors of preventing his promotion in filed claim
item.description: A former Registrar of Voters filed a claim against the county of San Diego, specifically making allegations against two sitting members on the Board of Supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Biden says he doesn't know whether Israel is holding up peace deal to influence 2024 US election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden had terse words for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday, and said he didn't know whether the Israeli leader was holding up a Mideast peace deal in order to influence the outcome of the 2024 U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Republicans embrace ‘pro-choice’ label, infuriating Democrats
item.description: A few Republicans running for competitive or Democratic-leaning congressional seats are adopting and reviving a label that was nearly extinct in their party: pro-choice.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams's future in question as legal woes threaten to mount
item.description: Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams is fighting for his political life as he finds himself in the middle of a legal firestorm, raising questions about how much longer he can hold on to power. Adams was indicted last week by the Manhattan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs arrested during traffic stop: NYPD
item.description: EAST HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York state Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs was arrested during a traffic stop in Manhattan on Thursday, according to the NYPD. The East Harlem Democrat was taken into custody in front of 1844 Lexington Ave. around 1:30 p.m., police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: President Joe Biden has toured some of the areas hardest-hit by the storm this week as he and Vice President Kamala Harris traveled to different parts of the Southeast. Former President Trump was in Georgia on Monday and criticized the federal response.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Bipartisan California lawmakers urge House leaders to boost disaster relief funding
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers from Orange County, Calif., are urging House leaders to include disaster relief funding in fiscal 2025 spending, highlighting wildfire damage in the state even as damage from Hurricane Helene on the other side of the country has heightened attention on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Early voting begins in downtown Chicago
item.description: Early voting for the November election begins Thursday in downtown Chicago, with DuPage County seeing nearly double the amount of voters who turned out early in 2020, and early voting in all 50 wards opens up next Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: NYC Schools Chancellor Banks to step down sooner than expected
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Schools Chancellor David Banks is expected to step down from his position sooner than expected, several months before he was supposed to retire, a City Hall spokesperson confirmed to PIX11 News on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Melania Trump in new memoir calls abortion a 'woman's fundamental right': Report
item.description: “Why should anyone other than the woman herself have the power to determine what she does with her own body?" asks the former first lady.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: NJ lawmakers explore why summer energy bills were so high
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - If your energy bills were sky-high this summer, you are not alone. It's why New Jersey lawmakers came together for a hearing to learn why and to discuss how to solve it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Here are some important election deadlines for the Las Vegas valley
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Nevada voters have until Oct. 22 to register to vote should they want a mail-in ballot, and remember, in-person registration is available on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minneapolis grants consent for Blue Line extension through city’s north side
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council signed off Wednesday on the proposed route of a light rail line that will run through a section of the city’s north side. However, some council members said they could only get to a yes vote after some of their concerns with the route were addressed.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: A court filing unsealed Wednesday argues that the former president is not entitled to immunity from prosecution over his failed bid to remain in power.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What we gleaned from key moments of the Walz-Vance debate in New York
item.description: MPR News senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson is in New York with takeaways from the only debate between the vice presidential hopefuls.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Dana Ferguson, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Soomaalida St. Cloud waxay rabaan in ay ka dhex muuqdaan siyaasadda, laakiin iyaga oo dhan isku aragti ma aha
item.description: In ka badan labaatameeyo sano ka hor ayey qaxootigan oo ka soo jeeda Bariga Afrika soo dageen St. Cloud, oo qaarkood ayaa raadinaya in ay door ku yeeshaan maamulka iyo siyaasadda.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, Matt Sepic, and Nicole Ki, MPR News

item.title: Walz’s account of son’s brush with gun violence offers poignant moment in VP debate
item.description: “Look, I’ve got a 17-year-old and he witnessed a shooting at a community center playing volleyball,” Walz said during the debate, referencing his son Gus. “Those things don’t leave you.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state of Minnesota came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate Ohio Sen. JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
